agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform handling shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing brackets.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A domain entity representing a parcel moving from a pickup address to a delivery address, progressing through defined lifecycle states."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where a parcel is collected from the sender."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where a parcel is delivered to the recipient."},{"term":"Shipment State","definition":"The current lifecycle phase of a shipment: Pending, In Transit, or Delivered."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"A pricing classification derived from parcel weight: Standard (< 10 kg), Heavy (≥ 10 kg and < 50 kg), or Freight (≥ 50 kg)."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for parcels under 10 kg."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for parcels weighing 10 kg up to but not including 50 kg."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for parcels weighing 50 kg or more, up to the 1000 kg maximum."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment can only be created when both a pickup address and a delivery address are provided.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without both endpoints is logically meaningless — it cannot represent a real-world delivery. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create a shipment missing either pickup or delivery address must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Shipment state transitions follow a strict forward-only lifecycle: Pending → In Transit → Delivered. No backward transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Backward state transitions (e.g., In Transit → Pending) would corrupt the shipment's audit trail and violate physical reality — a parcel already moving cannot un-move.","testable_assertion":"Transitioning a shipment from In Transit to Pending must be rejected with an error."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Delivered is a terminal state. Once a shipment reaches Delivered, no further state changes are allowed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Delivery is a physical finality — the parcel has been handed over. Allowing state changes after delivery would break downstream processes (billing, proof of delivery, customer notifications).","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change the state of a Delivered shipment must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly positive (> 0 kg) and must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zero or negative weight is physically impossible for a real parcel. The 1000 kg ceiling is a hard operational constraint (vehicle capacity, insurance, handling equipment).","testable_assertion":"Creating or updating a shipment with weight ≤ 0 or weight > 1000 must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Weight bracket Standard applies when weight < 10 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket thresholds are a business pricing policy that could be adjusted by management.","testable_assertion":"A shipment weighing 9.99 kg must be classified as Standard."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Weight bracket Heavy applies when weight ≥ 10 kg and weight < 50 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket thresholds are a business pricing policy that could be adjusted by management.","testable_assertion":"A shipment weighing exactly 10 kg must be classified as Heavy; a shipment weighing 49.99 kg must also be classified as Heavy."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Weight bracket Freight applies when weight ≥ 50 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Bracket thresholds are a business pricing policy that could be adjusted by management.","testable_assertion":"A shipment weighing exactly 50 kg must be classified as Freight; a shipment weighing 1000 kg must also be classified as Freight."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A request to create a shipment with a valid pickup address and delivery address","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The shipment is persisted in Pending state with both addresses recorded."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A request to create a shipment missing a pickup address or delivery address","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request and returns a validation error indicating the missing address."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"It is transitioned to In Transit","then":"The shipment state becomes In Transit."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"An attempt is made to transition it back to Pending","then":"The system rejects the transition and returns an error indicating backward transitions are not allowed."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"It is transitioned to Delivered","then":"The shipment state becomes Delivered."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A shipment in Delivered state","when":"Any state change is attempted","then":"The system rejects the change and returns an error indicating Delivered is a terminal state."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A shipment creation or update request with weight 0, negative, or exceeding 1000 kg","when":"The request is processed","then":"The system rejects it with a validation error specifying the weight constraint."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A shipment with weight 9.99 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The bracket is Standard."},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 10 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The bracket is Heavy."},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A shipment with weight 49.99 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The bracket is Heavy."},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 50 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The bracket is Freight."},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 1000 kg","when":"The weight bracket is determined","then":"The bracket is Freight and the shipment is accepted (at the upper bound)."},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"An attempt is made to transition directly to Delivered","then":"The system rejects the transition as it skips the In Transit state."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight at exact bracket boundaries (10 kg, 50 kg)","expected_behavior":"10 kg is Heavy (inclusive lower bound), 50 kg is Freight (inclusive lower bound). No gap or overlap between brackets."},{"scenario":"Weight of exactly 1000 kg","expected_behavior":"Accepted as valid and classified as Freight."},{"scenario":"Weight of 1000.01 kg","expected_behavior":"Rejected — exceeds the maximum allowed weight."},{"scenario":"Weight of 0.01 kg (minimum positive)","expected_behavior":"Accepted as valid and classified as Standard."},{"scenario":"Pending shipment attempts direct transition to Delivered (skipping In Transit)","expected_behavior":"Rejected. The lifecycle requires Pending → In Transit → Delivered in sequence."},{"scenario":"Delivered shipment attempted transition back to In Transit","expected_behavior":"Rejected. Delivered is terminal — no state changes of any kind are permitted."},{"scenario":"Shipment created with empty-string addresses","expected_behavior":"Should be treated the same as missing addresses and rejected, depending on validation rules for 'provided'."},{"scenario":"Duplicate creation with identical addresses","expected_behavior":"Each shipment is a distinct entity; duplicates in addresses are allowed as they represent different parcels."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Weight bracket classification directly drives pricing. Incorrect boundary handling (e.g., classifying a 50 kg parcel as Heavy instead of Freight) results in revenue leakage. State integrity ensures billing events fire at the correct lifecycle point.","operational":"Strict forward-only state machine prevents chaotic re-processing of parcels. Address validation at creation prevents failed pickups/deliveries that waste driver time and fuel.","user":"Customers receive predictable pricing based on transparent weight brackets. Shipment state visibility (Pending → In Transit → Delivered) sets clear delivery expectations.","risk":"Low overall risk — the spec is clear and well-bounded. Main risk is boundary precision in weight bracket classification (floating-point edge cases). Terminal state enforcement is critical to prevent billing and audit anomalies.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Shippers (Customers)","impact":"Pricing transparency and accuracy; trust that shipment state reflects reality."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Clean state machine prevents parcels from being re-dispatched or lost in invalid states."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Correct weight bracket assignment ensures accurate invoicing. Terminal Delivered state triggers final billing."},{"group":"Drivers / Couriers","impact":"Valid addresses at creation ensure pickable and deliverable parcels; no wasted trips."}]},"confidence":{"level":92,"reasoning":"The product manager's specification is explicit and internally consistent. State transitions are clearly defined with no ambiguity. Weight brackets have explicitly stated inclusive/exclusive boundaries. The only minor gap is whether Pending can skip directly to Delivered (inferred as disallowed from the forward-only lifecycle, but not explicitly stated).","high_confidence_areas":["State machine transitions and terminal state","Weight validation bounds (positive, max 1000 kg)","Weight bracket boundaries (explicitly stated inclusive/exclusive)","Address requirement for shipment creation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether Pending → Delivered (skipping In Transit) is allowed — inferred as disallowed but not explicitly stated","Definition of a 'valid' address (format, completeness) — not specified beyond 'provided'","Whether weight can be changed after shipment creation"],"assumptions":["Pending → Delivered (skipping In Transit) is not allowed, as the lifecycle is sequential","Weight is set at creation and the bracket is derived, not independently assigned","Addresses are non-null and non-empty strings at minimum"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
